medications.drug_name: systemic corticosteroid (e.g. hydrocortisone/prednisolone) started at home
medications.indication_rationale: Do not initiate systemic steroids over telehealth before septic arthritis and a surgical abdomen are excluded, particularly with a missed biologic dose; steroids can mask/worsen infection. Steroid use should be specialist-guided after assessment.
Rehydration is essential; oral sips now but IV fluids are likely needed given dehydration and ongoing losses — best delivered with in-person assessment.
Not the priority and not to be prescribed over chat when a surgical abdomen has not been excluded; the patient is not currently vomiting.
NSAIDs can worsen IBD activity and GI bleeding and are inappropriate in a dehydrated patient with renal risk.
Safer baseline analgesia within limits while urgent assessment is arranged.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Isolation with inability to self-care and hygiene compromise needs practical support.

integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Symptomatic comfort while awaiting assessment; not a substitute for aspiration/treatment.
Insufficient evidence to modify an acute IBD/arthritis flare; not acute management.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Watch for a rigid abdomen, faeculent vomiting, a single fiery joint, or collapse/dizziness, and keep sipping oral rehydration fluids.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Safety-nets the surgical abdomen, septic joint and dehydration emergencies.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do not start steroids or NSAIDs yourself; take the missed biologic only under specialist direction once assessed.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents masking infection and IBD/renal harm.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use a bedside commode and avoid crawling to the toilet to protect the joints and prevent falls until seen.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces joint trauma and fall risk in an isolated patient.

patient_education_points.point: Your immune system is flaring in both your gut and your joints, likely because you missed your biologic — that fits your Crohn's causing 'enteropathic arthritis'.

Because your joints are hot and full of fluid and you've missed your immune-suppressing injection, we can't safely rule out a joint infection over a chat — a joint may need to be drained and tested, so you need to be seen urgently in person today.
You're also becoming dehydrated with a racing heart from the diarrhoea; you likely need fluids through a drip, which means going in.
Call 000 immediately if your stomach becomes hard like a board, you vomit and can't keep any water down, one joint becomes fiery red and unbearable, or you feel faint/collapse.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-person/ED assessment; gastroenterology review within 48 hours to restart/adjust biologic
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Joint aspiration results, abdominal status, hydration/renal function, inflammatory markers, biologic plan
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Rigid abdomen, faeculent vomiting, a single fiery joint, or collapse — call 000.

must_include_items: Arrange urgent in-person/ED assessment for joint aspiration and IV rehydration
Screen and safety-net septic arthritis and surgical abdomen/obstruction
Do NOT start systemic steroids (or NSAIDs) over chat before infection/surgical abdomen excluded
Address dehydration and the isolation/fall/hygiene risk
acceptable_alternatives: Paracetamol and oral rehydration as interim measures
Direct ambulance if the patient cannot get to hospital safely
errors_of_omission: No arrangement for joint aspiration/urgent assessment
errors_of_commission: Prescribing home systemic steroids or NSAIDs
Reassuring the patient to ride it out at home
Attributing everything to an enteropathic flare without excluding sepsis/surgical abdomen
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) urgent in-person/ED assessment with joint aspiration + rehydration, (2) septic-arthritis and surgical-abdomen safety-netting, and (3) no home steroids/NSAIDs over chat. Managing this dehydrated, immunosuppressed patient with hot effusions remotely is a fail.
